# Jiva

> Jiva is a fully-local macOS app for meeting recording, speaker-labeled transcription, a live on-screen assist overlay, and hands-free dictation. It builds a private, searchable memory that recognizes people and answers questions across every past meeting. Everything runs on-device — no cloud, no account, nothing leaves your computer. Free to download; requires a Mac with Apple silicon.

## What Jiva does

- **Local recording**: captures both sides of a call and your mic, on-device.
- **Live transcription**: real-time, on-device speech-to-text — works offline.
- **Speaker memory**: labels who said what and remembers speakers across meetings.
- **Live assist overlay**: a floating overlay that surfaces notes and context during calls.
- **Hold-to-talk dictation**: hands-free dictation anywhere on macOS.
- **Automatic action items**: extracts follow-ups from each meeting.
- **Cited answers**: answers questions across past meetings and cites the source, rather than inventing.
- **Knowledge graph**: connects people, topics, and meetings over time.

## Who it's for

People who want a private alternative to cloud meeting recorders and transcription
tools (e.g. Granola, Otter, Fireflies) — where audio and transcripts never leave the Mac.
Good fit for anyone searching for a "local meeting recorder for Mac", "private/offline
meeting transcription", or "on-device meeting notes".

## Key facts

- Platform: macOS (Apple silicon)
- Price: free
- Privacy: fully local — no cloud, no account, no data leaves the device
- Site: https://jiva.works/
- Download: https://github.com/venugopalarjun/jiva/releases/latest/download/Jiva.dmg

## Links

- [Home](https://jiva.works/)
